title: podman-remote: build: creates a weird 'localhost/latest' image
body:
Usual setup for podman system service
```console
$ mkdir a
$ echo 'FROM docker.io/library/alpine:latest' >a/Containerfile
$ ./bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK build --layers=true -t test a
--> a24bb401329
a24bb4013296f61e89ba57005a7b3e52274d8edd3ae2077d04395f806b63d83e

FROM docker.io/library/alpine:latest
COMMIT test
Successfully built a24bb4013296f61e89ba57005a7b3e52274d8edd3ae2077d04395f806b63d83e
$ ./bin/podman images
REPOSITORY                TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE
localhost/test            latest  a24bb4013296  12 days ago  5.85 MB
docker.io/library/alpine  latest  a24bb4013296  12 days ago  5.85 MB
localhost/latest          latest  a24bb4013296  12 days ago  5.85 MB
^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
That last `localhost/latest` is totally weird and unexpected, and is causing failures in e2e test

comments.body: this is NOT a remote problem ... it happens with `podman build` ... still checking.
